cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: Context, not the AI model, now determines whether marketing stack spend pays off
stories.plain: New research argues the AI model itself is no longer the differentiator — the data context wrapped around it is. What separates winning stacks from stalled ones is real-time signals, machine-readable brand assets, and organizational upkeep. This reframes how buyers should evaluate and budget AI tools: infrastructure and data quality beat model choice.

stories.head: Pega's Tara DeZao explains how agentic AI shifts marketing from tool to autonomous orchestrator
stories.plain: A detailed practitioner interview with Pega's marketing AI lead covers agentic AI as end-to-end marketing orchestration — systems that understand intent, manage multi-step workflows, and give strategic feedback rather than just producing content on demand. Relevant for anyone deciding how to structure an AI-driven marketing operation.

stories.head: Cresta launches AI-powered training simulator to coach contact-centre agents with synthetic customers
stories.plain: Cresta's new simulator uses AI agents posing as realistic customers, built from real conversation data, to let human agents practice before going live. The pitch is faster onboarding and lower churn. It matters because it shows agentic AI moving into workforce training, a cost centre where ROI is measurable and adoption pressure is high.

stories.head: Infobip acquires SocketLabs to add intelligent email routing and observability to its platform
stories.plain: Infobip buys SocketLabs, gaining vendor-agnostic email observability and intelligent routing built over nearly 20 years. This consolidates enterprise email infrastructure under a larger CEP player, which affects how brands evaluate deliverability and channel reliability when choosing or renewing engagement platforms.

stories.head: Tec-Do integrates advertisers into ChatGPT Ads; Omneky opens MCP ad-creative server
stories.plain: Two signals in one news digest: Tec-Do gives advertisers a path into ChatGPT's ad inventory, and Omneky's MCP server lets AI agents call ad-creative generation as a tool. Together they show the paid-media supply chain adapting to generative and agentic AI, changing where and how brands buy and produce ads.

stories.head: Gartner: Customers 3x more likely to resolve issues via ChatGPT than company chatbots
stories.plain: Gartner research shows the function spending the most on AI is delivering the least return, and customers now prefer third-party AI tools over brand-owned chatbots by 3-to-1. This directly challenges where most CX AI budgets have gone and forces a rethink of where to deploy AI investment to actually serve customers.

stories.head: Context, not the model, now determines AI marketing ROI
stories.plain: Emerging research argues the AI model itself is no longer the differentiator — the data, real-time signals, and machine-readable brand context wrapped around it are what separate profitable AI stacks from stalled ones. Critical reading for anyone deciding where to spend on AI infrastructure or evaluating vendor claims.

stories.head: Demand-Genius: Only 16% of AI responses cite brands directly; citation rate is 0% in early buyer journey
stories.plain: New B2B research finds that AI engines almost never name brands during awareness and consideration stages, meaning standard visibility metrics badly misrepresent actual AI influence. Marketers measuring citation counts are tracking the wrong thing — conversational context shapes recommendations long before any explicit mention appears.

stories.head: AI adoption in CX hits 90% but governance and architecture choices are sharply divided
stories.plain: Near-universal AI adoption in customer experience masks a growing split in how organizations architect, govern, and earn customer trust from these systems. The divergence in approaches is starting to produce measurable outcome gaps, making governance and deployment architecture the new competitive battleground rather than adoption itself.

stories.head: Omneky launches public API and MCP server for autonomous ad creative generation
stories.plain: Omneky has opened its AI creative engine to developers and AI agents via a public API and Model Context Protocol server, letting any platform send brand data and receive campaign-ready ad creative back. This shifts autonomous creative generation from a closed product to embeddable infrastructure, changing how ad creative gets built into agentic stacks.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: Agentic AI Takes the Wheel in Marketing Orchestration
body: The conversation has moved well past prompts and chatbots: AI agents are now being deployed to orchestrate entire marketing workflows, from ad creative generation to customer-service training simulations. Pega's Tara DeZao and others frame this as a fundamental shift in who — or what — holds the process together.

Platforms are racing to expose agent-friendly surfaces. Omneky's launch of a public API & MCP server, ZoomInfo's GTM.AI CLI, and Cresta's training simulator all point to a world where autonomous agents are first-class citizens in the marketing stack, not bolt-on features. Decisions about tooling must now account for agent interoperability, not just human usability.
ev: Agile Brand · Agentic orchestration with Pega
MarTech Series · Omneky API & MCP launch

themes.head: AI Search Is Rewriting Visibility Rules
body: When AI systems answer questions directly, brand mentions in links no longer guarantee discovery. Trust signals — authoritative content, verified data, consistent brand voice — are becoming the primary currency that determines whether an AI recommends you or ignores you. Marketers optimising for click-through rates alone are measuring the wrong thing.

Conversational context now shapes recommendations in ways that keyword rankings never did. The shift demands a rethink of what 'being found' even means — and forces teams to audit how their brand appears inside AI reasoning chains, not just on search-results pages. Every measurement framework built on impressions and clicks is now partially obsolete.
ev: Agile Brand · Conversational context & brand recs

themes.head: The ROI Reckoning: AI Spend Meets Hard Scrutiny
body: After two years of experimentation budgets, executives are demanding proof that AI investments move revenue, not just efficiency metrics. Crunchbase CRO Ann Davis articulates what many CFOs are now saying privately: the bar for AI ROI is rising faster than most martech vendors anticipated, and 'we saved hours' is no longer sufficient justification.

A parallel problem is misallocated effort — teams using AI to scale the wrong parts of go-to-market while customer-acquisition costs keep climbing. Context determines cost and outcome, meaning the same AI capability can be transformative or wasteful depending on where it is deployed. The next budget cycle will separate experimentation theatre from genuine leverage.
ev: Agile Brand · Crunchbase CRO on AI ROI pressure
MarTech · Scaling the wrong part of GTM
Agile Brand · Context decides stack cost

themes.head: The CMS & Data Layer Becomes the AI Operating System
body: Content management and product-data systems are quietly becoming the control plane for AI-driven experiences. When AI generates storefronts, personalises pages, and populates agent responses, the quality and structure of underlying content data becomes a direct competitive variable — not a back-office concern. The Stibo & CommerceIQ partnership illustrates how product data gaps translate immediately into lost retail sales.

Infrastructure consolidation reflects the same logic: Infobip's acquisition of SocketLabs signals that reliable, high-volume data pipes matter more than ever when AI systems are sending communications at machine speed. Marketers who treat their data layer as legacy plumbing will find AI outputs are only as good as the content fed into them.
ev: MarTech · CMS as AI OS for brands
Agile Brand · Stibo & CommerceIQ product data gap

themes.head: Human Skill Atrophy: Who Trains Tomorrow's Marketer?
body: As AI handles more execution — writing copy, analysing data, coding prototypes — the junior roles that once built foundational skills are disappearing or being bypassed. The apprenticeship pipeline is breaking: if AI does the work, early-career marketers never develop the judgment needed to supervise AI well. This is a slow-motion capability crisis that will take years to surface fully.

The concern echoes across disciplines: 'vibe coding' scaling articles note that better prompts are not a substitute for engineering rigour, and CX deployment paths are diverging precisely because teams differ wildly in underlying human expertise. Organisations that invest in structured AI-era training — like Cresta's agent-training simulator — are betting that human judgment remains the scarce resource worth developing.
ev: MarTech · Scaling vibe coding isn't better prompts

month.takeaway: Across every layer of the stack, the defining tension of this moment is that AI is advancing faster than the human judgment, data infrastructure, and measurement frameworks needed to deploy it well — and the organisations that close that gap first will set the competitive standard for the rest of the decade.
